c74object.name: voice-alligator-audio~
c74object.digest: audio-to-audio voice allocator with built-in ADSR and glide
c74object.description:
voice-alligator-audio~ is the audio-input companion to voice-alligator~. Rather than receiving MIDI-style messages, it is driven entirely by signals: a trigger impulse, a pitch signal, and a velocity signal.
Notes are triggered by a rising edge (i.e. a click~) on inlet 1. Pitch and velocity are sampled at the moment of the trigger. Note duration is set via the duration attribute; after that many milliseconds the note enters its release phase automatically.
The ADSR attributes, glide attributes, and MC signal outlets are identical to voice-alligator~. See that reference for full documentation of the shared signal processing behaviour.
c74object.discussion:
Inlet 1 — trigger signal
A rising edge (transition from 0 to any non-zero value) fires a note-on.
Inlet 2 — pitch signal
When scale is off (default): a raw frequency in Hz. When scale is on: a MIDI note number (rounded to the nearest integer and looked up in the internal scale array, identical to voice-alligator).
Inlet 3 — velocity signal
Velocity sampled at the moment of the trigger.
MC Signal Outlets
Outlet 1 — freq~
Frequency in Hz.
Outlet 2 — env~
ADSR envelope output, velocity-scaled. Peak equals the normalised velocity from inlet 3.
Outlet 3 — impulse~
A single sample impulse fired at the start of each non-glide note attack. Set glide_impulse to also fire on glide notes.
Outlet 4 — ramp~
A linear ramp from 0.0 at note-on to 1.0 at the end of the release phase. The total ramp length equals duration + release in milliseconds. Useful for driving time-based modulations that span the full lifetime of a note. Outputs 0.0 when the voice is inactive.
Outlet 5 — glide flag~
1.0 while a glide is in progress, 0.0 otherwise.
Outlet 6 — mono flag~
1.0 while the voice is in mono mode, 0.0 otherwise.
Note duration and voice recycling
After duration milliseconds, the note-off is triggered internally on the audio thread. The ADSR release phase then runs through completely before the voice is returned to the pool.
Mono mode
When mono is on, all triggers share a single persistent voice. Each trigger glides to the new pitch rather than allocating a new voice. If the mono voice is still in its release phase when a new trigger arrives, the behaviour depends on mono_steals_release: if off (default), a fresh poly voice is allocated and the release rings out; if on, the releasing voice is stolen and glides to the new pitch.
ADSR parameter changes
ADSR parameter changes take effect on the next note-on only. Currently playing voices are not affected.

method.name: scale_def
method.description:
Identical to voice-alligator. Only relevant when scale is on.
See the voice-alligator reference for full documentation.

method.name: print
method.digest: Print active voices and all parameter settings to console
method.description: Prints active voices, inactive voices, and the current value of every parameter to the Max console.

attribute.name: duration
attribute.description:
How long each note plays before its release phase begins, in milliseconds. Sampled at the moment of each trigger, so changes take effect on the next note.
default: 1000 ms

attribute.name: active
attribute.description:
When off, incoming triggers are ignored. ADSR of already-playing voices is unaffected.
default: true

attribute.name: mono
attribute.description:
When on, all triggers share one persistent voice. Each trigger glides to the new pitch. See mono_steals_release for behaviour when a trigger arrives during the release phase.
default: false

attribute.name: mono_steals_release
attribute.digest: Steal releasing mono voice on new trigger (default false)
attribute.description:
Controls what happens when a new trigger arrives while the mono voice is still in release.
0: The release rings out and the new note is allocated on a fresh voice.
1: The releasing voice is stolen and immediately glides to the new pitch.
default: false

attribute.name: steal
attribute.description:
When on, if no free voice is available the oldest active voice is stolen.
default: true

attribute.name: scale
attribute.digest: Use scale array for pitch lookup (default false)
attribute.description:
When off (default), inlet 2 is treated as a raw frequency in Hz.
When on, inlet 2 is rounded to the nearest integer and used as a MIDI note number to look up a frequency in the internal scale array. Use scale_def to define the tuning.
default: false

attribute.name: basefreq
attribute.description:
Used as the reference frequency for the default MTOF scale table.
default: 440.0

attribute.name: attack
attribute.description:
Attack time in milliseconds. Takes effect on the next note-on.
default: 10 ms

attribute.name: sustain_level
attribute.description:
Sustain level as a fraction of peak (0–1). The actual sustain amplitude is sustain_level × normalised velocity.
default: 0.8

attribute.name: attack_curve
attribute.description:
0 is linear, positive values are convex (fast start), negative values are concave (slow start). Range: -1 to 1.
default: 0

attribute.name: decay_curve
attribute.description:
Same range and convention as attack_curve.
default: 0

attribute.name: release_curve
attribute.description:
Same range and convention as attack_curve.
default: 0

attribute.name: declick_ms
attribute.description:
When a voice is stolen, a declick ramp of this duration is applied before the new attack begins.
default: 5 ms

attribute.name: glidetime
attribute.description:
Time in milliseconds for outlet 1 to glide from the previous pitch to the new pitch. 0 = instant snap.
default: 30 ms

attribute.name: glide_curve
attribute.description:
Same range and convention as attack_curve.
default: 0

attribute.name: glide_retrigger
attribute.digest: Retrigger envelope on glide notes (default false)
attribute.description:
0 (legato): the envelope runs uninterrupted through the glide.
1 (retrigger): the envelope restarts from the current level with the new velocity applied.
default: false

attribute.name: glide_impulse
attribute.digest: Fire impulse on glide notes (default false)
attribute.description:
When on, outlet 3 fires a 1-sample impulse on glide notes as well as fresh note-ons. When off (default), glide notes produce no impulse.
default: false

attribute.name: scale_def_mode
attribute.digest: Scale definition mode: 0 MIDI, 1 frequency (default frequency)
attribute.description:
Determines how values in a scale_def message are interpreted.
default: 1 (frequency)

attribute.name: scale_fill
attribute.digest: Fill undefined scale entries with MTOF values (default true)

attribute.name: debug
attribute.description:
Prints trigger, voice allocation, and ADSR completion events to the Max console.
default: false
